Notice Of Motion To Deem Plaintiffs' Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set Two Admitted, Or In The Alternative, Move To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two
Off Calendar Apr-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Ferline P. Ruiz, Esq. of TUCKER ELLIS & WEST (415-617-2209), appearing for defendant / moving party Carrier Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs /opposing parties Cheryl Hoskins, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S Motion To Deem Plaintiffs' Responses To Requests For Admissions, Set Two Admitted, Or In The Alternative, Move To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission, Set Two. Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling: Defendant Carrier Corporation's motion to compel further responses to request for admissions, set two, is granted as to requests 48 and 50-55. The requests are relevant on the issue of Proposition 51 allocation and are not vague, ambiguous, burdensome, harassing, make-work or of no utility. Defendant withdrew As to Request 49. Both Plaintiff and Defendant's requests for sanctions are denied. Prevailing party is to prepare an order for the court's signature. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported. = (220/HEK)
